title: Capstone Case Study 1 - Phase 2 Business Blueprint & Solution Architecture

sections.content: The requirement gathering workshops are complete. The customer has approved the project scope. Your next responsibility is to design the SAP TM solution before configuration begins.

sections.background: The Procurement Manager wants planners to always select the cheapest carrier. The Logistics Director disagrees because delayed deliveries cost more than transportation savings.
questions: Should cost be the primary selection criterion?
How should SAP TM rank carriers?
What happens when the cheapest carrier has no available vehicles?

sections.title: Transportation Strategy
sections.question: Which planning strategy should be implemented?

options.text: Choose the carrier based on SLA, capacity, cost and performance.
Allow planners to manually decide every shipment.

sections.explanation: SAP TM optimization should evaluate multiple constraints rather than only transportation cost. This provides the best overall business outcome.

sections.subject: Transportation Budget
sections.body: The Board has reduced the implementation budget by 15%. We cannot implement every requested feature during Phase 1. Please recommend which capabilities should be included immediately and which can be postponed.

sections.title: Solution Architect Advice
sections.content: A successful blueprint balances business requirements, implementation effort, project budget and future scalability. Avoid including every requested feature in the initial Go-Live if it increases project risk.

sections.title: Accenture Solution Design Question
sections.content: A client requests all SAP TM features in Phase 1, but the budget is reduced by 15%. How would you respond? Prioritize business-critical capabilities, defer lower-value enhancements, explain the impact to stakeholders and document the roadmap for future phases.

sections.title: Phase 2 Complete
sections.content: The Business Blueprint has been approved. The project is ready to move into the Realize phase, where SAP TM configuration, integration development and unit testing will begin.
